problem: A score is shown as a bare number with no indication of its scale (e.g. "out of 100") or what it measures, leaving the reader to guess whether higher is better and what the number represents.
evidence: Startkorpus item from issue #511 ("Score ohne Skala oder Bedeutung"); addressed structurally by src/registry/metrics.rs::MetricSpec, whose mandatory unit/direction/needs_explanation/docs_url fields exist specifically so every registered metric carries a documented scale and meaning, cross-referenced from docs/OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md's per-metric anchors.
expected: Every score-like metric that reaches the JSON or PDF has a MetricSpec entry in the registry documenting its unit, direction, and a docs_url anchor explaining what it measures.
regression: tests/registry_contract.rs asserts every registry entry's docs_url resolves to an existing anchor in docs/OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md and every band_set reference resolves.
counter_examples: A count/ratio field that is intentionally not score-like (e.g. a raw occurrence count with no banding) is registered with band_set = None and must not be forced into a banded-score shape.
